free_text: Experimental studies and nuclear model calculations
on the formation of radioactive products in
interactions of medium energy protons with copper, zinc
and brass:  estimation of collimator activation in
proton therapy facilities.
The cyclotron of the Svedberg
laboratory.  Incident proton energes - 180 and 130 MeV.
The Saturne National Laboratory.
Incident proton energies - 250 and 350 MeV.
Copper - foil, various thicknesses, purity 99.9%.
Zinc - fine powder, purity 99.999%. The powder was
pressed to obtain pellets of suitable thicknesses.
Brass - foil, 100 mumeters thick, purity 99.9%.
For e< 200 MeV only.
For e>200 MeV.
Proton beam current is to be 40-80 nanoamperes
at Uppsala.
Several stacks containing Zn pellets, and copper
and brass foils were irradiated. Seven stacks
containing up to 40 target foils each were irradiated
at Uppsala.  Six Thin Stacks were Irradiated at Saclay.
Irradiation time was to be 4-8 hours.
For study of low yield products, radiochemical
separations were performed.
The photopeak area analysis was done assuming a
gaussian shape.
- By authors - the formation of secondary particle
fields could generally be neglected for proton energies
below 200 MeV;  for irradiations at Saturne the
secondary particle influence was minimized by the use
of thin stacks.
The primary proton energies were determined
by time-of-flight measurements.
Half of the energy absorbed in each target
foil was adopted as uncertainty.
It is combination in quadrature of all
uncertainties:
1. The total uncertainty in proton beam current was
estimated to be 10-15%,
2. The uncertainty in the peak area analysis was
generally 5-10%, in extremely weak samples, however, up
3. The uncertainty of detector calibration was to be
4. The uncertainty of chemical separation yield was to
Comparation of integrated and integral cross
section for the formation of a radionuclides in the
interaction of brass with protons.
Theoretical calculations by ALICE-IPPE code.
By F.C.
The additions which were proposed by
O.Schwerer. (See his e-mail 18-mar-2004). Updated.
Last checking has been done.
